process.name: Certified Container Weighing
process.description: Capturing the weight of full containers for IMO SOLAS VGM compliance

stakeholders.customer: Owner of the container, represented by the booth visitor
stakeholders.serviceProvider: The company doing the weighing and issuing the weighing certificate

steps.name: Start
steps.description: Before we start weighing your container, let us get to know each other so we can contact you back. Enter your details to book the weighing.

event.name: booking.created
event.summary: The weighing request enters the shared process and the shipper becomes visible to the service provider.

steps.name: Container Intake
steps.description: The container arrives at the weighing location. Record the container number if available and decide what should happen next.

fields.label: Container number
fields.hint: Optional. If you enter one, the ISO 6346 control digit is checked.

quiz.question: A container arrives at the weighing location. What should happen next?

choices.label: Request the customer to confirm the container number
Capture the container number and associate it with the customer or owner
Contact the ocean carrier and the customer to announce that a container will be weighed
Nothing. Just move on to the next step

quiz.correctFeedback: Correct. Intake is where the container identity gets linked to the shipper in a trustworthy way.
quiz.incorrectFeedback: The key process-sharing move here is to capture the container identifier and associate it with the customer or owner.

event.name: container.intake.recorded
event.summary: An intake event ties the physical container to the booking so downstream actors can trust they are talking about the same asset.

steps.name: Container Weighing
steps.description: The container is weighed and a weighing certificate is prepared. The service provider generates the verified gross mass and exposes it as a process event.

quiz.question: The container is weighed. What should happen next?

choices.label: The container should be stored at the weighing location awaiting payment by the customer
Since the certificate is issued to the carrier automatically, the container can be delivered at the terminal
The container can move on to the terminal, but the weight should be reported to the carrier before arriving there
Contact the ocean carrier and the customer to report the weight of the container

quiz.correctFeedback: Correct. The certificate matters because the weight must be available to the carrier before terminal delivery becomes risky.
quiz.incorrectFeedback: The process still has to share the weight with the carrier before the container reaches the terminal.

event.name: container.weighed
event.summary: A weighing event is issued with the measured VGM, time, and reference so the next actors can act on trusted data.

steps.name: Certificate Purchase
steps.description: The weight certificate can now be purchased by the customer. Answer the certificate-content question to continue the process.

quiz.question: Which data points should be included in the weighing result?

choices.label: Container ID, weight, timestamp and reference
All of the above with a picture of the scale

quiz.correctFeedback: Correct. The useful shared event is compact but traceable: container ID, weight, timestamp and reference.
quiz.incorrectFeedback: The essential shared weighing result is container ID, weight, timestamp and reference.

event.name: certificate.purchase.confirmed
event.summary: The customer confirms the certificate purchase and the commercial side of the process becomes visible in the same journey.

steps.name: Issue Certificate
steps.description: You did it. Your container can now be brought safely on board. Choose the best next action once the certificate exists.

quiz.question: Congratulations, you have obtained a weighing certificate. What should you do now?

choices.label: Send an email to the carrier to report the weight for the container
Issue an event in the ocean shipping process with the weight certificate
Nothing. Certiweight will report it to the carrier directly

quiz.correctFeedback: Correct. In the strongest setup the process event is issued and downstream reporting can become automated from that shared truth.
quiz.incorrectFeedback: The core PILOTS angle is to issue a process event with the certificate so trusted automation becomes possible.

event.name: certificate.issued
event.summary: The weighing certificate becomes a shareable process event that can trigger the next operational steps across company boundaries.
dataPoints: Final state Purchased to Issued / Done
